description: Fix three contributor experience issues found during platform audit: (1) DEP0190 deprecation warnings in dev mode and unit tests from unconditional shell:true child process spawning, (2) incorrect npm run check description in CONTRIBUTING.md that omits build:plugins and storybook:build steps, (3) misleading error message in opentabs-plugin build when tsc exits 0 but emits no files due to stale tsbuildinfo.

userStories.title: Fix DEP0190 deprecation warning in dev.ts from unconditional shell:true
userStories.description: Fix the Node.js DEP0190 deprecation warning in scripts/dev.ts by replacing the unconditional shell:true on execFileSync with an execSync call using a string command. The warning fires on every npm run dev on Linux/Mac and will become a fatal error in a future Node.js version.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/dev.ts no longer uses execFileSync with both args array and shell:true
Running npm run dev on Linux does not print DEP0190 warning during lefthook install step
lefthook install is still executed at dev startup (non-fatal if fails)

userStories.title: Fix DEP0190 deprecation warning in create-plugin test from unconditional shell:true
userStories.description: Fix the Node.js DEP0190 deprecation warning in platform/create-plugin/src/index.test.ts by making shell:true conditional on Windows only. The test unconditionally applies shell:true on all platforms even though the comment says it's only needed on Windows.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/create-plugin/src/index.test.ts uses shell:true only on process.platform === 'win32'
Running npm run test on Linux/Mac does not print DEP0190 warning from index.test.ts
The scaffolded plugin build test still passes on all platforms
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/create-plugin/src/index.test.ts (make shell:true conditional on Windows)

Context:
The test uses spawnSync with shell:true unconditionally. The comment explains it's for Windows .cmd scripts, but it fires DEP0190 on Linux/Mac too.

Current code (index.test.ts, lines 266-267):
```
// shell: true is required on Windows where npm is a .cmd script
const spawnOpts = { cwd: projectDir, env: buildEnv, shell: true } as const;
```
This spawnOpts object is used at lines 270, 278, 286, 291 (install, build, lint, format:check runs).

Changes:
Make shell:true conditional:
```
// shell: true is required on Windows where npm is a .cmd script
const spawnOpts = { cwd: projectDir, env: buildEnv, shell: process.platform === 'win32' } as const;
```
The `as const` still applies. Since `process.platform === 'win32'` evaluates to a boolean, this is type-safe.

Constraints:
- Only change line 267 — do NOT change any of the four spawnSync call sites (they just use spawnOpts)
- Do NOT change the comment on line 266
- Do NOT add any new import

userStories.title: Fix misleading error message in plugin-tools build when tsc emits no files
userStories.description: Improve the error message in platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts when tsc exits 0 but the expected entry point file is not created. The current message says 'Check your tsconfig.json outDir setting' but the most common actual cause is a stale tsconfig.tsbuildinfo file.
acceptanceCriteria: Error message at build.ts line 1084 mentions tsconfig.tsbuildinfo as the likely cause
Error message instructs the user to delete tsconfig.tsbuildinfo and retry
Error message still mentions tsconfig.json as a secondary check
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts (update error message at line 1084)

Context:
When a stale tsconfig.tsbuildinfo exists alongside a deleted dist/ directory, tsc considers everything up-to-date (incremental mode) and exits 0 without emitting any files. The build.ts error handler then throws a misleading message pointing to tsconfig.json. This is a known pain point: delete dist/, keep tsbuildinfo, run opentabs-plugin build — get 'Check your tsconfig.json outDir setting' which points to the wrong thing.

Current code (build.ts, line 1084):
```
throw new Error(`tsc succeeded but ${entryPoint} was not created. Check your tsconfig.json outDir setting.`);
```

Changes:
Update the error message to name the actual most common cause:
```
throw new Error(`tsc succeeded but ${entryPoint} was not created. If you deleted dist/ manually, delete tsconfig.tsbuildinfo and retry. Otherwise, check your tsconfig.json outDir setting.`);
```

Constraints:
- Only change this one string literal at line 1084 inside the if-block checking `!(await access(entryPoint)...)`
- Do NOT change surrounding code — the check, the try/catch, or any other error messages in this function
- The variable `entryPoint` must remain interpolated (it's the actual path, not a hardcoded string)

userStories.title: Fix npm run check description in CONTRIBUTING.md to include build:plugins and storybook:build
userStories.description: Update CONTRIBUTING.md to accurately describe what npm run check actually runs. The current description omits build:plugins (builds all 114 plugins, takes minutes, modifies plugin lockfiles) and storybook:build, causing contributor confusion about why check takes much longer than expected.
acceptanceCriteria: CONTRIBUTING.md inline comment for npm run check mentions build:plugins and storybook:build
CONTRIBUTING.md table row for npm run check accurately describes all steps
No other text in CONTRIBUTING.md is changed
userStories.notes:
Files:
- CONTRIBUTING.md (update two places that describe npm run check)

Context:
The actual npm run check script (package.json) is:
npm run build && npm run build:plugins && npm run storybook:build && npm run lint && npm run format:check && npm run knip && npm run test

Two places in CONTRIBUTING.md describe this incorrectly:

1. Inline comment (line 105):
```
npm run check       # build + lint + format:check + knip + unit tests
```
Missing: build:plugins and storybook:build

2. Table row (line 121):
```
| `npm run check`            | All root checks (build + lint + format:check + knip + unit tests) |
```
Missing: build:plugins and storybook:build

Changes:

1. Update line 105:
```
npm run check       # build + build:plugins + storybook:build + lint + format:check + knip + unit tests
```

2. Update line 121 table row:
```
| `npm run check`            | All root checks (build + build:plugins + storybook:build + lint + format:check + knip + unit tests) |
```

Constraints:
- Only change these two lines — do NOT restructure or reformat the surrounding table
- Do NOT add new rows or sections
- Preserve the exact column alignment style of the surrounding table rows (pad with spaces if needed to maintain the pipe-aligned format)
